extracted_information: Each state has its own laws and regulations for cannabis businesses. States can mandate specific types of insurance or bonds. Cities and counties may also have their own laws and insurance requirements. Beyond cannabis-specific rules, each state has general business insurance and licensing requirements. Some policies or bonds are government-mandated, while others are part of risk management. Factors affecting cannabis business insurance costs include business type, property/equipment/inventory value, revenue, location, number of employees, policy limits/deductibles, and claims history.

general_liability_limits.state: New Jersey (Hired & Non-Owned Auto for Delivery)

performance_bond_amounts.state: Florida (Medical Marijuana Treatment Centers serving 1000+ patients)

cost_factors: Value of business property, equipment, and inventory

features.name: General liability insurance
features.description: Protects against basic third-party risks such as customer injury, damage to customer property, libel, and advertising injuries.

features.name: Product liability insurance
features.description: Helps pay for legal costs if a client sues over an adverse reaction to a product, including allergic reactions, panic attacks, or other adverse effects.

features.name: Commercial property insurance
features.description: Helps with property damage to storefronts or business personal property, theft of merchandise, and vandalism. Often required by landlords.

features.name: Commercial auto insurance
features.description: Covers vehicle theft, vandalism, and accidents involving company vehicles. Most states require this for businesses that own vehicles.

features.name: Workers' compensation insurance
features.description: Protects against workplace illnesses or injuries for employees, covering medical expenses, disability benefits, and legal fees from lawsuits. Usually required for businesses with employees.

features.name: Surety bonds
features.description: Guarantees that the cannabis business will fulfill contractual terms and pay suppliers. Many states require as part of licensing requirements to ensure compliance with laws, fulfilled license requirements, and protection from breach of contract.

features.name: Finished stock insurance
features.description: Covers the value of cannabis inventory if it becomes damaged and unable to be sold, protecting against burglary, theft, fire, natural disasters, and vandalism.

features.name: Professional liability insurance
features.description: Covers claims of professional negligence. Specifically mentioned for testing laboratories in Montana.

features.name: Performance bond
features.description: A type of bond that guarantees completion of a contract or obligation. Mentioned in Florida and Utah.

features.name: Financial assurance/Escrow account
features.description: Alternative to or alongside bonds, to ensure financial responsibility.

state_specific_requirements.cannabis_legal_status: Legal for medical use
state_specific_requirements.requirements: Cultivators and integrated facilities are required to maintain $2 million in liability and casualty insurance, as well as workers' compensation insurance.

state_specific_requirements.requirements: Each licensed premise must have a $5,000 surety bond. Distributors must carry general liability insurance with a $1 million per-occurrence limit and a $2 million aggregate limit.

state_specific_requirements.requirements: General liability insurance / product liability insurance: $1 million per-occurrence, $2 million aggregate, $5,000 maximum deductible. Surety bond: Equal to the business's license fee.

state_specific_requirements.requirements: Product liability insurance: $100,000 bodily injury liability. General liability insurance: $100,000 premises liability. Auto liability insurance for vehicles used to transport marijuana products.

state_specific_requirements.requirements: Applicants must submit a business plan that includes a plan for obtaining liability insurance. Cannabis delivery vehicles must have hired and non-owned auto insurance (HNOA) in an amount of not less than $1 million per occurrence or accident.
